interpretation: At the outbreak of World War I, multiple belligerent symbolic orders achieved maximum mobilizational strength by invoking the nation as a sacred collective body that dissolved all internal distinctions. Kaiser Wilhelm's dissolution of party divisions and Poincaré's 'sacred union' both demonstrate the mechanism: the symbolic order expands to absorb all individuals into a single sacrificial body at the moment of perceived existential threat.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Passages are well-attested verified texts. The mechanism is well-supported for the mobilization moment. Confidence capped given multi-nation unit of analysis.

mechanismSummary: At war outbreak, national symbolic orders in Germany and France suppressed all internal distinctions and sacralized the nation as a collective body demanding total sacrifice — Kaiser Wilhelm recognized 'only Germans,' Poincaré invoked 'sacred union.'
